Description: Initialize project structure, Docker, CI/CD pipeline
Create project folder structure, virtual environment, and install base dependencies
List all Python packages: fastapi, uvicorn, sqlalchemy, langchain, chromadb, mlflow, deepeval, prometheus-client
Define environment variables: DATABASE_URL, SECRET_KEY, OLLAMA_BASE_URL, MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI
Exclude .venv, __pycache__, .env, chroma_db, mlruns from version control and Docker image
Multi-layer Dockerfile: python:3.12-slim base, system deps, PyTorch CPU-only, pip install, healthcheck, CMD uvicorn
Define 4 services: api, db (postgres), prometheus, grafana with volumes, networking, healthchecks
postgres:16-alpine image, port 5433, named volume for data persistence, healthcheck with pg_isready
Add prometheus + grafana containers, mount config files, auto-provision datasource and dashboard

Summary: Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline
Description: Workflow: lint with flake8 → run pytest → build Docker image on push to main
PostgreSQL schema, user model, JWT auth system
Create database.py with engine, SessionLocal, Base; config.py with pydantic-settings for DATABASE_URL
SQLAlchemy User model (id, username, email, hashed_password, created_at) + Pydantic schemas for request/response

Summary: Create Query model for audit trail
Description: SQLAlchemy Query model (id, user_id, query, reponse, created_at) with foreign key to User
auth_service.py: password hashing with bcrypt, JWT creation with python-jose, token verification
POST /auth/register: validate input, check duplicate username/email, hash password, save to db
POST /auth/login: OAuth2 form data, verify password, return JWT access_token with 30-min expiry
FastAPI Depends() that extracts and validates JWT from Authorization header, returns User object
Document ingestion, hybrid retrieval, reranking, generation
Load PDF with PyPDFLoader, split with RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter (chunk_size=1000, overlap=150)
Embed chunks with paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2, store in ChromaDB with persist_directory
Tokenize all chunks, create BM25Okapi index, rebuild on each new PDF upload
Track indexed filenames in a set, skip already-indexed PDFs, return status: indexed or already_exists
Query both ChromaDB (k=8 dense) and BM25 (k=8 sparse), merge results, deduplicate by page_content
Score all candidate chunks with cross-encoder/mmarco-mMiniLMv2-L12-H384-v1, keep top 3 most relevant
Use Mistral to rewrite follow-up questions into standalone queries using last 3 conversation turns

Summary: Implement prompt building and LLM generation
Description: Build medical assistant prompt in French with context + history, call Mistral 7B via Ollama (temperature=0)
French medical assistant system prompt: answer only from context, say 'Information non trouvée' if not found
Read OLLAMA_BASE_URL from env, default to localhost:11434, Docker uses host.docker.internal:11434

Summary: Create RAG service bridge for FastAPI
Description: rag_service.py: connect FastAPI routes to rag_engine, save queries to db, orchestrate MLflow + DeepEval calls
POST /rag/ask (authenticated), POST /rag/upload-pdf (authenticated), wire to rag_service
MLflow logging, DeepEval quality metrics, experiment comparison
Configure mediassist-rag experiment, log_rag_query function with all params/metrics/artifacts
Log 20+ params: chunk_size, overlap, embedding_model, k_dense, k_sparse, k_final, llm_model, temperature, top_p, top_k
Log response_time_ms, query_length, answer_length, num_sources per query run
Log de_answer_relevancy, de_faithfulness, de_contextual_relevancy, precision_at_k, recall_at_k
Save full query/answer/sources/scores as JSON artifact attached to each MLflow run
deepeval_service.py: compute AnswerRelevancyMetric, FaithfulnessMetric, ContextualRelevancyMetric using LLM-as-judge
Set DEEPEVAL_LLM to use Ollama Mistral instead of OpenAI, handle scoring errors gracefully
Implement precision@k and recall@k as lightweight approximations when DeepEval is unavailable
Prometheus metrics, Grafana dashboard, healthchecks
Install prometheus-fastapi-instrumentator, call Instrumentator().instrument(app).expose(app) in main.py

dashboards.yml: auto-load JSON dashboards from /var/lib/grafana/dashboards on startup
GET /health returns 200 OK, used by Docker HEALTHCHECK and load balancers
